title: What are UFOs? Eric Weinstein on the various branches of the decision tree for UFOs. #ufo
summary: Eric Weinstein analyzes the 2021 UAP report delivered to the Intelligence Committee, breaking down the decision tree structure used to categorize unidentified aerial phenomena. He notes the apparent internal conflict within the report between disclosure advocates and those resistant to revealing more information.

acronyms.definition: The official term used by government and military for mysterious aerial sightings, replacing the colloquial term UFO

key_claims.claim: The Intelligence Committee ordered the Director of National Intelligence and Secretary of Defense to deliver a report on UAP sightings
The UAP report contained detectable internal conflict between voices wanting to disclose more and those resistant to disclosure
The report's decision tree categorizes UAP as either unintentional (clutter, atmospheric effects) or intentional, with intentional further divided into US origin, known others, and unknown others
The 'unknown others' category refers to intentional aerial phenomena that are not from the US or any known party, but not necessarily aliens
